headlines.title: Oil Refiners Are Cashing In on a Market That Won’t Stay Broken
headlines.summary: Oil refiners have stumbled into one of the best profit environments in years. Crude prices have fallen back to where they traded before the Iran war erupted. But gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel remain stubbornly expensive. That combination has pushed refining margins to extraordin

headlines.title: America Extends Its Lead Over Russia and Saudi Arabia in Oil Output
headlines.summary: While the United States has been the world’s biggest consumer of oil for decades, it became the largest oil producer, too, back in 2018, and has retained that crown ever since. headlines.title: OPEC Cuts Demand Forecast Again as the Oil Market Starts Looking Past Hormuz
headlines.summary: OPEC has trimmed its 2026 global oil demand growth forecast for the third straight month, even as crude production rebounds across the Gulf and tanker traffic slowly returns to the Strait of Hormuz. headlines.title: Why the UAE Is Taking a Bigger Piece of America’s LNG Crown Jewel
headlines.summary: As might be expected in the zero-sum game of global energy, there is much more than meets the eye to the acquisition by Abu Dhabi-headquartered international investment company XRG of an additional equity interest in Trains (self-contained production facilities) 4 and 5 of the U.

headlines.title: Oil Prices Surge 8% After Trump Reimposes Iran Blockade
headlines.summary: Oil prices jumped more than 8% on Monday after U.S. President Donald Trump announced the United States would reinstate its blockade on Iran, forcing traders to once again price in the risk of prolonged disruption to energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz. headlines.title: EU Drops Sanctions on Lukoil Founder and Russian Patriarch
headlines.summary: The European Union will not impose sanctions on the head of the Russian Orthodox Church and the chief of Russia’s largest private oil company, after Bulgaria vetoed the proposal. headlines.title: Iraq’s Oil Lifeline Has Been Saved, But For How Long?
headlines.summary: The clock that was ticking down to economic disaster for Iraq on 27 July has been paused, with Turkey agreeing to a one-year temporary arrangement that ensures the continuation of critical oil exports running via pipelines from northern Iraq into the Turkish port of Ceyhan. headlines.title: IEA Chief Slams Europe’s Slow Electrification as Energy Crisis Deepens
headlines.summary: The head of the International Energy Agency has warned Europe made a “major mistake” by failing to end its economy reliance on imported fossil fuels quickly enough since the 2022 energy crunch. Fatih Birol said Europe’s low electrification rate, electricity’s share of the energy

headlines.title: EU's Russian LNG Imports Hit Record High Ahead of 2027 Ban
